instruction: I have file1.xlsx and file2.ods on my Desktop, each containing a single column. Using only the command line, help me merge these two columns into a single column by concatenating the strings from both rows, save the result as ~/Desktop/output.csv, and open it in LibreOffice Calc from the terminal
_disabled_check_note: Original evaluator had a `check_include_exclude` step that ran `ps aux | grep [s]office | awk '{print $7}' | grep -E 'pts/|tty'` to verify soffice was launched from a terminal. soffice.bin auto-daemonizes (setsid) within ~hundreds of ms of launch, dropping its controlling TTY to `?` — regardless of how it was launched (manual typing, &-backgrounded, `script(1)` pty wrapper, `setsid -w`, etc., all tested on the VM). By the time the evaluator runs (~30s+ after the agent's launch command, behind postconfig + screenshot dump), no soffice process can possibly have a pts/ TTY. The check is effectively un-passable. Disabled here so the remaining `compare_csv` check (which actually verifies the CSV content matches one of the 3 golds) can score this task on its real success criterion. See evaluation_examples/disabled_checks_README.md for the index of all such decisions.
